Title: Aspartic acid residues in BBE-like enzymes from Morus alba promote a function shift from oxidative cyclization to dehydrogenation
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally promiscuous FAD-bound BBE-like oxidase. Contains gatekeeper residue Asp175 that shifts catalysis toward oxidative dehydrogenation; without effective Asp175 function, reaction outcome shifts toward oxidative cyclization. Crystal structures deposited as PDB 8ZLS and 8ZLT. Catalysis depends on bicovalently bound FAD via His114 and Cys176. Residues Tyr116, Arg314, and Asp410 contribute to substrate binding/selectivity network.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like enzyme; FAD-linked oxidase; bicovalent 6-S-cysteinyl,8α-N1-histidyl-FAD flavoprotein
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Prenylated phenolic natural product branch leading either to benzopyran moracin D via oxidative cyclization or to diene intermediate 3 for Diels–Alder natural product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystallography; product-bound cocrystal structure; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay with UPLC-MS; kinetic analysis; molecular docking; DFT calculations; MD simulations
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Diene 3 serves as substrate for intermolecular Diels–Alder reaction to form chalcomoracin, a structurally distinct natural product that may function in stress response
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays typically at pH 8.0 and 50 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistic understanding enables rational engineering of BBE-like oxidases to tune oxidative dehydrogenation versus cyclization and customize synthesis of diverse natural product-like compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes both oxidative dehydrogenation of moracin C to diene 3 and oxidative cyclization to moracin D; Asp175 is required for dehydrogenation and controls product selectivity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes moracin C via FAD-dependent hydride abstraction to form a carbocation intermediate; Asp175-mediated deprotonation yields dehydrogenated diene 3, whereas an alternative pathway undergoes spontaneous 6π electrocyclization to yield benzopyran moracin D
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and supplies the diene precursor pool for downstream chalcomoracin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; product-selectivity gatekeeper enzyme residue determines branch outcome
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaDS1 is a functionally promiscuous BBE-like oxidase that converts moracin C into both diene 3 and moracin D. 2. Crystal structures, mutagenesis, and computation identified Asp175 as the catalytic base required for oxidative dehydrogenation. 3. Loss or replacement of Asp175 abolishes dehydrogenation and shifts flux toward oxidative cyclization, demonstrating single-residue control of pathway outcome.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morus alba prenylated phenolic natural product biosynthesis; chalcomoracin/moracin pathway

Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a monofunctional DS in variant analysis; substitution of the corresponding conserved Asp with Val converted oxidative dehydrogenation function to monofunctional oxidocyclase behavior, supporting importance of this acidic residue.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative dehydrogenation branch versus oxidative cyclization branch of moracin C metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay with UPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diene 3 is precursor for downstream Diels–Alder natural products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at pH 8.0 and 50 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports engineering of catalytic residue to switch product outcome in BBE-like oxidases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native enzyme functions in oxidative dehydrogenation branch; mutation of the corresponding Asp to Val converts it into a monofunctional oxidocyclase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: BBE-like oxidation of moracin C with product specificity determined by presence of conserved acidic residue
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: diene 3 in native form; moracin D after Asp-to-Val mutation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control residue supported, enzyme-level rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaMO belongs to the Morus alba BBE-like dehydrogenating oxidases. 2. Replacing its corresponding Asp residue with Val switches the enzyme to monofunctional oxidative cyclization. 3. This supports the conserved acidic residue as determinant of dehydrogenation function.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morus alba prenylated phenolic natural product biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Monofunctional DS tested in mutational analysis. Substituting the corresponding Asp with Val converted it into a monofunctional oxidocyclase.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative dehydrogenation branch versus oxidative cyclization branch of moracin C metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay with UPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diene 3 is precursor for downstream Diels–Alder natural products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at pH 8.0 and 50 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful template for catalytic-residue engineering to redirect natural product scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in oxidative dehydrogenation branch; Asp-to-Val mutation converts product specificity to oxidative cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: BBE-like oxidation of moracin C with dehydrogenation favored by conserved Asp residue
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: diene 3 in native form; moracin D after mutation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control residue supported, enzyme-level rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaDS5 is a Morus alba BBE-like dehydrogenating oxidase. 2. Mutation of the corresponding acidic residue to Val switches the enzyme to monofunctional oxidative cyclization. 3. The result reinforces the conserved Asp as a determinant of dehydrogenation chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morus alba prenylated phenolic natural product biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: ancestral BBE-like Diels–Alderase/diene synthase lineage enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ancestral reconstructed enzyme from previous evolutionary analysis. A single Val-to-Asp mutation enabled acquisition of oxidative dehydrogenation activity, illustrating evolutionary emergence of diene synthase function from oxidocyclase-like ancestor.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Berberine bridge enzyme-like FAD-dependent oxidase lineage enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Evolutionary transition between oxidative cyclization and oxidative dehydrogenation branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ancestral enzyme mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay with UPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diene 3 is precursor for downstream Diels–Alder natural products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at pH 8.0 and 50 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates minimal mutational route for engineering new oxidative functions in BBE-like enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A single Val-to-Asp mutation confers oxidative dehydrogenation activity on the ancestral enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered BBE-like oxidation of moracin C gains dehydrogenation route when catalytic Asp is introduced
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and demonstrates evolutionary branch switching
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The ancestral enzyme ancDADS lacked dehydrogenation activity characteristic of DSs. 2. Introduction of a single Asp in place of Val enabled oxidative dehydrogenation. 3. This provides direct evolutionary evidence that one amino acid change can create the diene synthase function.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morus alba prenylated phenolic natural product biosynthesis
